source: The conversation of the men assembled here, turned upon the neighbouring land, and farmers; and when those topics were exhausted, upon the age of some old man who had been buried on the previous Sunday; the young men present considering him very old, and the old men present declaring him to have been quite young—not older, one white-haired grandfather said, than he was—with ten or fifteen year of life in him at least—if he had taken care; if he had taken care.
domestication: 聚在这里的男人们聊起了附近的土地和农夫们，这些话题聊完后，又开始谈论上个星期天埋葬的一个老人的年纪。在场的年轻人觉得他很老了，但老一辈的人却说他还年轻，一个满头白发的老大爷还说，他跟自己差不多大，还能再活个十年八年呢，要是他自己注意身体的话，要是他自己注意身体的话。
foreignization: 这里聚集的男人们的谈话转向了邻近的土地和农民；当这些话题耗尽时，又转向了前一个周日下葬的一位老人的年龄；在场的年轻人认为他非常老，而在场的老人们则宣称他其实很年轻——并不比其中一位白发祖父更老，这位祖父说那位老人至少还有十年或十五年的寿命——如果他注意保养的话；如果他注意保养的话。

source: Alas! How few of Nature’s faces are left alone to gladden us with their beauty! The cares, and sorrows, and hungerings, of the world, change them as they change hearts; and it is only when those passions sleep, and have lost their hold for ever, that the troubled clouds pass off, and leave Heaven’s surface clear. It is a common thing for the countenances of the dead, even in that fixed and rigid state, to subside into the long-forgotten expression of sleeping infancy, and settle into the very look of early life; so calm, so peaceful, do they grow again, that those who knew them in their happy childhood, kneel by the coffin’s side in awe, and see the Angel even upon earth.
domestication: 唉！如今自然界的美好面容所剩无几，能让我们心生欢喜的更是寥寥无几！世上的忧愁、烦恼和饥饿，改变着人们的面貌，就像它们改变人心一样；只有当这些激情消散，永远失去了掌控，那些忧云才会散去，天空才会再次清澈。常见的是，即使在死者的脸上，即使是在那固定而僵硬的状态下，面容也会逐渐恢复到久违的婴儿般的安睡表情，重新呈现出早年生活的模样；它们变得如此平静、安宁，以至于那些曾在他们快乐童年时认识他们的人，会在棺材旁跪下，怀着敬畏之心，仿佛看到了人间的天使。
foreignization: 唉！自然的面容中有多少还保持着它们的美丽来让我们高兴啊！世间的忧虑、悲伤和饥饿改变了它们，就像它们改变心灵一样；只有当这些激情沉睡，永远失去了它们的控制时，纷扰的乌云才会消散，留下天堂表面的清澈。即使是死者的面容，在那种固定和僵硬的状态下，也常常会恢复到早已遗忘的婴儿睡眠时的表情，重新呈现出早年生活的模样；它们再次变得如此平静，如此安宁，以至于那些在他们幸福童年时认识他们的人，会在棺材旁跪下，敬畏地看到天使甚至在人间。

source: Now, although it was very natural that the board, of all people in the world, should feel in a great state of virtuous astonishment and horror at the smallest tokens of want of feeling on the part of anybody, they were rather out, in this particular instance. The simple fact was, that Oliver, instead of possessing too little feeling, possessed rather too much; and was in a fair way of being reduced, for life, to a state of brutal stupidity and sullenness by the ill usage he had received. He heard the news of his destination, in perfect silence; and, having had his luggage put into his hand—which was not very difficult to carry, inasmuch as it was all comprised within the limits of a brown paper parcel, about half a foot square by three inches deep—he pulled his cap over his eyes; and once more attaching himself to Mr. Bumble’s coat cuff, was led away by that dignitary to a new scene of suffering.
domestication: 现在，董事会的人对任何人表现出一点点冷漠就大惊小怪、义愤填膺，这本是情理之中的事。但这一次，他们可真是错得离谱了。其实，奥利弗的问题不是感情太少了，而是太多了。他受到的虐待已经把他往麻木不仁、闷闷不乐的路上推，估计这辈子都好不了了。听到自己要去的地方，他一声不吭；行李也不过是半尺见方、三寸厚的小包，拿在手里轻飘飘的。奥利弗把帽子拉低，遮住眼睛，再次抓住邦布尔先生的衣袖，跟着这位大人物前往又一个受苦的地方。
foreignization: 现在，虽然对于世界上所有人来说，董事会感到非常道德上的震惊和厌恶，对于任何人表现出的丝毫缺乏感情的迹象都感到非常自然，但在这个特定的例子中，他们却是错的。简单地说，奥利弗并不是因为缺乏感情，而是因为拥有过多的感情；他由于受到的虐待，正走向一条终生处于野蛮愚蠢和阴郁沉默的状态的道路。他听到自己去向的消息时，完全沉默；并且，在行李被放到他手里之后——这并不难携带，因为所有的东西都装在一个大约半英尺见方、三英寸厚的棕色纸包里——他把帽子拉到眼睛上；再次把手搭在邦布尔先生的衣袖上，由这位尊贵的人物带领着，前往一个新的受苦之地。

source: As Mr. Bumble spoke, he made a melancholy feint of grasping his lantern with fierce determination; and plainly showed, by the alarmed expression of every feature, that he did want a little rousing, and not a little, prior to making any very warlike demonstration: unless, indeed, against paupers, or other person or persons trained down for the purpose.
domestication: 布姆先生说着话，假意做出一副悲壮的样子，紧紧握住灯笼，似乎决心已定；但从他脸上每一道惊恐的神情来看，显然他是需要打起精神的，而且是需要大大地振作一番，才能鼓足勇气去做出什么英勇的举动来。当然了，如果对象是穷人或是专门找来的靶子，那另当别论。
foreignization: 当邦布尔先生说话时，他做出了一副忧郁的样子，假装坚定地抓住了他的灯笼；并且显然通过每个特征的惊恐表情表明，他在做出任何非常好战的举动之前确实需要一点激发，而且是相当多的激发：除非，确实是针对穷人，或其他为此目的而受训的人。

“Why, look’e, young gentleman,” said Toby, “when a man keeps himself so very ex-clusive as I have done, and by that means has a snug house over his head with nobody a prying and smelling about it, it’s rather a startling thing to have the honour of a wisit from a young gentleman (however respectable and pleasant a person he may be to play cards with at conweniency) circumstanced as you are.”
瞧瞧，小少爷，托比说道，一个人像我这样把自己关得紧紧的，家里头清清静静，没人来烦来闹，突然有位小少爷大驾光临，尽管打牌时是个不错的伙伴，可也真是叫人心里一惊啊。

“Why, look’e, young gentleman,” said Toby, “when a man keeps himself so very ex-clusive as I have done, and by that means has a snug house over his head with nobody a prying and smelling about it, it’s rather a startling thing to have the honour of a wisit from a young gentleman (however respectable and pleasant a person he may be to play cards with at conweniency) circumstanced as you are.”
“喂，你看，年轻人，”托比说，“当一个人像我这样非常地与世隔绝，因此在头上有一所舒适的房子，而没有人来窥探和嗅探，突然有位年轻绅士（无论他多么体面，多令人愉快地在方便的时候一起打牌）在你这种情况下光临，还真是件让人吃惊的事。”

source: It was a dull, close, overcast summer evening. The clouds, which had been threatening all day, spread out in a dense and sluggish mass of vapour, already yielded large drops of rain, and seemed to presage a violent thunder-storm, when Mr. and Mrs. Bumble, turning out of the main street of the town, directed their course towards a scattered little colony of ruinous houses, distant from it some mile and a-half, or thereabouts, and erected on a low unwholesome swamp, bordering upon the river.
domestication: 那是个沉闷、潮湿、阴云密布的夏夜。天上的乌云一整天都憋着劲儿，终于凝成了厚厚的、慵懒的一团水汽，开始滴下大颗的雨珠，预示着一场猛烈的雷雨即将来临。正在这时，班布尔夫妇从镇上的主街上拐了出来，朝着距此大约一里半远的一片破败房屋走去。这些房子零零散散地建在河边一个低洼不健康的沼泽上。
foreignization: 那是一个沉闷、闷热、阴云密布的夏日傍晚。整天都威胁着要下雨的云层，现在扩展成一片浓重而迟缓的水汽，已经开始落下大滴的雨点，并似乎预示着一场猛烈的雷暴。就在这个时候，邦布尔先生和夫人从镇上的主街拐出，朝着离镇约一英里半左右的一片零散的破败房屋走去，这些房屋建在一个靠近河边的低洼不健康的沼泽上。

source: The same kind friend had, with Mr. Losberne’s assistance, cautiously stopped all channels of communication through which they could receive intelligence of the dreadful occurrences that had so recently taken place. “It was quite true,” he said, “that they must know them before long, but it might be at a better time than the present, and it could not be at a worse.” So, they travelled on in silence: each busied with reflections on the object which had brought them together: and no one disposed to give utterance to the thoughts which crowded upon all.
domestication: 那位好心的朋友在洛斯伯恩先生的帮助下，小心翼翼地堵住了所有他们可能得知最近发生的可怕事件的渠道。“他们迟早会知道这些事的，”他说，“但换个时间知道可能会更好，至少不会更糟。”于是，他们默默地继续赶路：每个人都在心里盘算着把他们聚在一起的原因：谁也不想说出此刻心头涌上的种种思绪。
foreignization: 同一位好心的朋友在洛斯伯恩先生的帮助下，小心翼翼地切断了所有他们可能通过以获知最近发生的可怕事件的消息的渠道。“确实，”他说，“他们迟早会知道这些事情，但也许现在不是最好的时机，而绝不会是最坏的。” 因此，他们默默地继续前行：每个人都在思考将他们聚集在一起的目的：没有人愿意表达涌上心头的想法。

source: “Why, do you mean to say you couldn’t have done it, if you had chosen?” demanded Monks, sternly. “Haven’t you done it, with other boys, scores of times? If you had had patience for a twelvemonth, at most, couldn’t you have got him convicted, and sent safely out of the kingdom; perhaps for life?”

source: With many adieus and good wishes, Mr. Fagin went his way. Noah Claypole, bespeaking his good lady’s attention, proceeded to enlighten her relative to the arrangement he had made, with all that haughtiness and air of superiority, becoming, not only a member of the sterner sex, but a gentleman who appreciated the dignity of a special appointment on the kinchin lay, in London and its vicinity.
domestication: 费金先生辞别众人，带着许多祝福上路了。诺亚·克莱波勒则摆出一副高高在上的架势，仿佛不仅是个男人，更是个懂得伦敦及其周边地区“小偷行业”特别任命之尊严的绅士，他向自己的好太太详细解释了所做的一切安排。
foreignization: 带着许多告别和美好的祝愿，费金先生走上了他的路。诺亚·克萊普尔请求他善良的夫人的注意，接着以一种不仅符合男性成员，而且符合一位认识到在伦敦及其周边地区担任儿童盗窃培训特别职务的尊严的绅士所应有的傲慢和优越感，向她阐明了他所作出的安排。

source: Mr. Losberne and Oliver had remained at another end of the apartment while this hurried conversation was proceeding. The former now held out his hand to Harry Maylie; and hearty salutations were exchanged between them. The doctor then communicated, in reply to multifarious questions from his young friend, a precise account of his patient’s situation; which was quite as consolatory and full of promise, as Oliver’s statement had encouraged him to hope; and to the whole of which, Mr. Giles, who affected to be busy about the luggage, listened with greedy ears.
domestication: 洛斯伯恩先生和奥利弗一直待在房间的另一头，趁着这场急促的对话进行。这时，洛斯伯恩先生伸出手来与哈利·梅利相握，两人热烈地问候了一番。接着，医生应年轻朋友的诸多询问，详细讲述了他的病人的状况。这情况正如奥利弗所述那般令人宽慰，充满了希望。吉尔斯先生假装忙着整理行李，却竖起耳朵听得津津有味。
foreignization: 洛斯伯恩先生和奥利弗在房间的另一端待着，当这场匆忙的对话进行时。前者现在向哈利·梅利伸出手；两人之间进行了热情的问候。医生随后回答了他年轻朋友的各种问题，详细地介绍了他的病人的状况；这情况完全像奥利弗的陈述让他希望的那样令人安慰且充满希望；而吉尔斯先生假装在忙于行李，却用贪婪的耳朵聆听了这一切。

source: At any other time, this remonstrance, and the tone in which it was delivered, would have had the desired effect; but the girl being really weak and exhausted, dropped her head over the back of the chair, and fainted, before Mr. Sikes could get out a few of the appropriate oaths with which, on similar occasions, he was accustomed to garnish his threats. Not knowing, very well, what to do, in this uncommon emergency; for Miss Nancy’s hysterics were usually of that violent kind which the patient fights and struggles out of, without much assistance; Mr. Sikes tried a little blasphemy: and finding that mode of treatment wholly ineffectual, called for assistance.
domestication: 换在别的时候，这番斥责和说话的语气肯定能奏效；但姑娘实在是虚弱得厉害，还没等西克斯先生骂出几句惯常用来威胁人的脏话，就头一歪，晕倒在椅子背上。遇到这种少见的情况，西克斯先生也慌了神；因为南希小姐平时发歇斯底里都是那种猛烈挣扎型的，不用别人怎么帮忙就能缓过来。于是，西克斯先生试着骂了几句，发现根本不管用，只好大声呼救。
foreignization: 在任何其他时候，这种责备以及说话的语气都会产生预期的效果；但女孩确实虚弱和疲惫，西克斯先生还来不及说出几个他惯常用来加强威胁的适当咒骂，她就头一歪倒在椅背上，昏了过去。面对这种不同寻常的紧急情况，西克斯先生不知所措；因为南希小姐的歇斯底里通常是那种病人会激烈挣扎着摆脱的类型，不需要太多帮助；西克斯先生试着来点亵渎神明的话：发现这种治疗方法完全无效后，便呼叫求助。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

At any other time, this remonstrance, and the tone in which it was delivered, would have had the desired effect; but the girl being really weak and exhausted, dropped her head over the back of the chair, and fainted, before Mr. Sikes could get out a few of the appropriate oaths with which, on similar occasions, he was accustomed to garnish his threats. Not knowing, very well, what to do, in this uncommon emergency; for Miss Nancy’s hysterics were usually of that violent kind which the patient fights and struggles out of, without much assistance; Mr. Sikes tried a little blasphemy: and finding that mode of treatment wholly ineffectual, called for assistance.
换在别的时候，这番斥责和说话的语气肯定能奏效；但姑娘实在是虚弱得厉害，还没等西克斯先生骂出几句惯常用来威胁人的脏话，就头一歪，晕倒在椅子背上。遇到这种少见的情况，西克斯先生也慌了神；因为南希小姐平时发歇斯底里都是那种猛烈挣扎型的，不用别人怎么帮忙就能缓过来。于是，西克斯先生试着骂了几句，发现根本不管用，只好大声呼救。

source: Muttering a curse upon his tardiness, Sikes pushed Oliver before him; and they entered a low dark room with a smoky fire, two or three broken chairs, a table, and a very old couch: on which, with his legs much higher than his head, a man was reposing at full length, smoking a long clay pipe. He was dressed in a smartly-cut snuff-coloured coat, with large brass buttons; an orange neckerchief; a coarse, staring, shawl-pattern waistcoat; and drab breeches. Mr. Crackit (for he it was) had no very great quantity of hair, either upon his head or face; but what he had, was of a reddish dye, and tortured into long corkscrew curls, through which he occasionally thrust some very dirty fingers, ornamented with large common rings. He was a trifle above the middle size, and apparently rather weak in the legs; but this circumstance by no means detracted from his own admiration of his top-boots, which he contemplated, in their elevated situation, with lively satisfaction.

source: “It is because I was your father’s oldest friend, young man,” returned Mr. Brownlow; “it is because the hopes and wishes of young and happy years were bound up with him, and that fair creature of his blood and kindred who rejoined her God in youth, and left me here a solitary, lonely man: it is because he knelt with me beside his only sisters’s death-bed when he was yet a boy, on the morning that would—but Heaven willed otherwise—have made her my young wife; it is because my seared heart clung to him, from that time forth, through all his trials and errors, till he died; it is because old recollections and associations filled my heart, and even the sight of you brings with it old thoughts of him; it is because of all these things that I am moved to treat you gently now—yes, Edward Leeford, even now—and blush for your unworthiness who bear the name.”
domestication: “我是你父亲最老的朋友，小伙子，”布朗洛先生回答道，“年轻时的希望和梦想都和他绑在一起，还有那个年轻就离世的女子，她本是他的血亲，却早早地回到了上帝的怀抱，留我一人孤苦伶仃；当年他还只是个孩子，却在我唯一的妹妹临终时跪在床边，那天本该是我们喜结连理的日子，可天不遂人愿；从那时起，我这颗伤痕累累的心就紧紧牵挂着他的命运，陪他走过了一次次的考验和错误，直到他去世；旧日的回忆和情谊填满了我的心，就连看见你，都会勾起我对他的思念；正因为这些原因，我才愿意对你手下留情——对，爱德华·利福德，即使是现在——也为你顶着这个名字而感到羞愧。”

source: “Never mind wot it is!” replied Sikes. “I want a boy, and he musn’t be a big ’un. Lord!” said Mr. Sikes, reflectively, “if I’d only got that young boy of Ned, the chimbley-sweeper’s! He kept him small on purpose, and let him out by the job. But the father gets lagged; and then the Juvenile Delinquent Society comes, and takes the boy away from a trade where he was earning money, teaches him to read and write, and in time makes a ’prentice of him. And so they go on,” said Mr. Sikes, his wrath rising with the recollection of his wrongs, “so they go on; and, if they’d got money enough (which it’s a Providence they haven’t,) we shouldn’t have half a dozen boys left in the whole trade, in a year or two.”

source: In a handsome room: though its furniture had rather the air of old-fashioned comfort, than of modern elegance: there sat two ladies at a well-spread breakfast-table. Mr. Giles, dressed with scrupulous care in a full suit of black, was in attendance upon them. The sickly rays of the suspended lantern falling directly upon them, aggravated the paleness and anxiety of their countenances: which, encircled by the deepest gloom and darkness, looked ghastly in the extreme.
雷声似乎实际上更近了，几乎就在他们的头顶上震颤和破裂，等这声音平息下来后，蒙克斯从桌子上抬起脸，向前倾身去听那个女人要说的话。三个人的脸几乎碰在一起，两个男人因为急切想听到而俯身在小桌上，女人也向前倾身以便让她的耳语可以被听见。悬挂在那里的灯笼发出的病态光线直射在他们身上，加剧了他们脸色的苍白和焦虑：在这最深的黑暗和阴霾中，他们的面容显得极其恐怖。

source: Good Heaven! what was that, which sent the blood tingling to his heart, and deprived him of his voice, and of power to move! There—there—at the window—close before him—so close, that he could have almost touched him before he started back: with his eyes peering into the room, and meeting his: there stood the Jew! foreignization: 天哪！那是什么，让他的血液直冲心脏，使他失去了声音和行动的能力！在那里——那里——在窗前——离他如此之近——如此之近，以至于他几乎可以在退缩之前碰到他：眼睛窥视着房间，与他的目光相遇：那里站着那个犹太人！而在他旁边，因愤怒或恐惧，或是两者皆有而脸色苍白的，是那个在客栈院子里搭讪他的男人那凶恶的面孔。

source: “I went,” said Mr. Brownlow, after a short pause, “I went, when all was over, to the scene of his—I will use the term the world would freely use, for worldly harshness or favour are now alike to him—of his guilty love, resolved that if my fears were realised that erring child should find one heart and home to shelter and compassionate her. The family had left that part a week before; they had called in such trifling debts as were outstanding, discharged them, and left the place by night. Why, or whither, none can tell.”

He remembered to have seen the waggons, as they went out, toiling up the hill. He took the same route; and arriving at a footpath across the fields, which he knew, after some distance, led out again into the road; struck into it, and walked quickly on.
他记得看到那些马车出门时，费力地爬上了山坡。他也沿着同一条路走去，走了一段后，拐进了一条他知道最终会回到大路上的田间小道，加快了脚步往前走。

source: Quickening their pace, they turned up a road upon the left hand. After walking about a quarter of a mile, they stopped before a detached house surrounded by a wall: to the top of which, Toby Crackit, scarcely pausing to take breath, climbed in a twinkling.
domestication: 他们加快了脚步，拐进了左手边的一条路。走了大约四分之一英里后，在一栋独立的房子前停了下来，这房子四周有围墙围着。托比·克拉基特几乎没喘口气，一眨眼就爬上了墙顶。
foreignization: 加快了脚步，他们向左手边的一条路拐去。走了大约四分之一英里后，他们在一幢被围墙环绕的独立房屋前停了下来：托比·克瑞基特几乎没有停下来喘口气，一眨眼就爬到了墙顶。

source: But, for many days, Oliver remained insensible to all the goodness of his new friends. The sun rose and sank, and rose and sank again, and many times after that; and still the boy lay stretched on his uneasy bed, dwindling away beneath the dry and wasting heat of fever. The worm does not work more surely on the dead body, than does this slow creeping fire upon the living frame.
domestication: 可是，奥利弗对新朋友们的好意迟迟无动于衷。太阳升起又落下，再升起再落下，反反复复许多回；可这孩子依然躺在床上，身体在高烧的炙烤下日渐消瘦。这缓慢蔓延的火苗侵蚀活人的肉体，就像虫子蛀蚀死尸一样，一点一滴地蚕食着生命。
foreignization: 但是，许多天过去了，奥利弗仍然对新朋友的所有好意毫无感觉。太阳升起又落下，再次升起又落下，之后又多次如此；而这个男孩依然躺在他那不舒适的床上，因发热的干燥和消耗性的热度而日渐消瘦。虫子不会比这种缓慢蔓延的火更确凿地在死尸上蛀蚀，就像它在活生生的身体上那样。

source: Fagin nodded to him to take no further notice just then; and, in a few minutes, the girl subsided into her accustomed demeanour. Whispering Sikes that there was no fear of her relapsing, Fagin took up his hat and bade him good-night. He paused when he reached the room-door, and looking round, asked if somebody would light him down the dark stairs.
domestication: 费金朝他点了点头，示意暂时别理会。没过多久，那女孩就恢复了平时的样子。费金低声对赛克斯说不用担心她会再发作，然后拿起帽子，道了声晚安。走到门口时，他停了下来，环顾四周，问有没有人能送他下楼，那楼梯可黑得很。
foreignization: 费金示意他暂时不要理会；几分钟后，女孩恢复了她平时的态度。费金低声对西克斯说不用担心她会再犯，然后拿起帽子和他道晚安。当他走到房门口时停了下来，环顾四周，问是否有人可以给他照明下黑暗的楼梯。

source: Although Oliver had enough to occupy his attention in keeping sight of his leader, he could not help bestowing a few hasty glances on either side of the way, as he passed along. A dirtier or more wretched place he had never seen. The street was very narrow and muddy, and the air was impregnated with filthy odours.
domestication: 虽然奥利弗忙着跟着领路人，但还是忍不住匆匆扫视了两旁。这地方脏得没法再脏，穷得没法再穷，他从没见过这么糟糕的地方。街道又窄又泥泞，空气中弥漫着一股股恶臭。
foreignization: 虽然奥利弗在跟上领路人时已经足够忙碌，但他还是忍不住匆匆向两边瞥了几眼。他从未见过比这更脏更凄惨的地方。街道非常狭窄泥泞，空气中弥漫着污秽的气味。

source: Oliver was surprised to see that as Mrs. Maylie said these words, she checked her lamentations as though by one effort; and drawing herself up as she spoke, became composed and firm. He was still more astonished to find that this firmness lasted; and that, under all the care and watching which ensued, Mrs. Maylie was ever ready and collected: performing all the duties which had devolved upon her, steadily, and, to all external appearances, even cheerfully. But he was young, and did not know what strong minds are capable of, under trying circumstances. How should he, when their possessors so seldom know themselves?
domestication: 奥利弗惊讶地发现，梅利太太说完这些话，仿佛使了什么力气，立刻止住了悲伤；说话间挺直了身子，变得镇定自若。更让他吃惊的是，这种镇定一直持续着；在随后的忙碌和照料中，梅利太太始终从容不迫，尽心尽力地完成了所有职责，看上去甚至还有些高兴。可他年轻，哪懂得在困境中坚强的心能有多大能耐呢？别说他不懂，就连那些有这样心灵的人，自己也往往搞不清楚啊。
foreignization: 奥利弗惊讶地看到，梅利太太在说出这些话时，仿佛通过一次努力就抑制住了她的哀叹；并且在说话时挺直了身子，变得镇定而坚定。他更加惊讶地发现，这种坚定持续了下来；在随后的所有照料和看护中，梅利太太始终准备充分且冷静：坚定地履行了所有落在她身上的职责，从外表上看，甚至是愉快的。但他还年轻，不知道坚强的心灵在困难情况下能有多大的能力。他怎么会知道呢，即使这些心灵的拥有者自己也常常不清楚？

source: “I repeat that I firmly believe it. To prove to you that I am disposed to trust you, I tell you without reserve, that we propose to extort the secret, whatever it may be, from the fear of this man Monks. But if—if—” said the gentleman, “he cannot be secured, or, if secured, cannot be acted upon as we wish, you must deliver up the Jew.”
domestication: 我再说一遍，我坚信不疑。为了证明我对你们的信任，我毫无保留地告诉你们，我们打算利用这个叫蒙克斯的家伙的恐惧，逼他说出那个秘密，不管那是什么。但是，如果——如果——”那位先生说，“他要么抓不住，要么抓住了也达不到我们的目的，那你们就得把那个犹太人交出来。
foreignization: 我重申，我坚信这一点。为了向你表明我是愿意信任你的，我不加保留地告诉你，我们打算利用这个名叫蒙克斯的人的恐惧来勒索出那个秘密，不管它是什么。但是，如果——如果——”这位先生说，“他无法被控制，或者即使被控制了，也无法按照我们的意愿行事，你就必须交出那个犹太人。”

source: Oliver ate his share, and the Jew then mixed him a glass of hot gin and water, telling him he must drink it off directly, because another gentleman wanted the tumbler. Oliver did as he was desired. Immediately afterwards he felt himself gently lifted on to one of the sacks; and then he sunk into a deep sleep.

source: “Ay, I do,” returned the girl, meeting his gaze. “I shall be glad to have him away from my eyes, and to know that the worst is over. I can’t bear to have him about me. The sight of him turns me against myself, and all of you.”

“Ay, I do,” returned the girl, meeting his gaze. “I shall be glad to have him away from my eyes, and to know that the worst is over. I can’t bear to have him about me. The sight of him turns me against myself, and all of you.”

source: They were happy days, those of Oliver’s recovery. Everything was so quiet, and neat, and orderly; everybody so kind and gentle; that after the noise and turbulence in the midst of which he had always lived, it seemed like Heaven itself. He was no sooner strong enough to put his clothes on, properly, than Mr. Brownlow caused a complete new suit, and a new cap, and a new pair of shoes, to be provided for him. As Oliver was told that he might do what he liked with the old clothes, he gave them to a servant who had been very kind to him, and asked her to sell them to a Jew, and keep the money for herself. This she very readily did; and, as Oliver looked out of the parlour window, and saw the Jew roll them up in his bag and walk away, he felt quite delighted to think that they were safely gone, and that there was now no possible danger of his ever being able to wear them again. They were sad rags, to tell the truth; and Oliver had never had a new suit before.
domestication: 那是奥利弗康复的日子，真是快乐啊！一切都那么安静，整洁，有序；每个人都那么和善温柔。和他以前生活中的嘈杂与混乱相比，这里简直像是天堂一般。他刚一有力气穿衣服，布朗洛先生就让人给他准备了一身全新的衣服，还有新帽子和新鞋子。奥利弗被告知旧衣服可以随意处置，他就把这些衣服给了一个对他很好的仆人，让她拿去卖给犹太人，卖的钱归她自己。仆人高兴地答应了。奥利弗从客厅的窗户往外看，看到那个犹太人把衣服卷进包里，扬长而去，心里别提多高兴了，终于不用担心再穿上那些破烂衣服了。说真的，那些衣服破得可怜，奥利弗以前可从未穿过新衣服呢。

source: The man who growled out these words, was a stoutly-built fellow of about five-and-thirty, in a black velveteen coat, very soiled drab breeches, lace-up half boots, and grey cotton stockings which inclosed a bulky pair of legs, with large swelling calves;—the kind of legs, which in such costume, always look in an unfinished and incomplete state without a set of fetters to garnish them. He had a brown hat on his head, and a dirty belcher handkerchief round his neck: with the long frayed ends of which he smeared the beer from his face as he spoke. He disclosed, when he had done so, a broad heavy countenance with a beard of three days’ growth, and two scowling eyes; one of which displayed various parti-coloured symptoms of having been recently damaged by a blow.
domestication: 这人大声吼出这几句话，是个壮实的汉子，约莫三十来岁，穿一件黑天鹅绒外套，脏兮兮的浅色马裤，系带半靴，灰棉袜子裹着一双粗腿，小腿肚鼓鼓的——这种腿，穿上这样的行头，不配上一副脚镣，总显得有点儿没完成似的。他头上戴一顶棕色的帽子，脖子上围了条脏兮兮的大手帕，说话时还用那条破烂的长边擦脸上的啤酒。这么一擦，露出一张宽大的脸，满是三天没刮的胡子，两只凶巴巴的眼睛，其中一只五颜六色的，显然是最近挨了一拳。
foreignization: 说出这些话的那个人，是个大约三十五岁左右、身材结实的家伙，穿着黑色天鹅绒外套，非常脏的浅黄色马裤，系带半靴，以及灰色棉袜，包裹着一对粗大的腿，有着明显膨胀的小腿肚——这种装扮下的双腿，如果没有一副镣铐来装饰，总是显得未完成和不完整。他头上戴着一顶棕色帽子，脖子上围着一条脏兮兮的花格手帕：说话时，他用这手帕长长的磨损的两端擦去脸上的啤酒。他这样做完后，露出了一张宽大沉重的脸庞，长着三天的胡须，还有两只怒视的眼睛；其中一只眼睛显现出各种五颜六色的迹象，表明最近受过打击。
